paragraph: The single-family market in Hillsdale is a real seller's market. Fifty-two homes sold over eight months at a median of $870,000, with 67 percent of them closing above asking price and a sale-to-list ratio of 1.045. Median days on market sits at 14 days, which means priced homes are moving fast and sellers are dictating terms. The range spans from $510,000 to $1,750,000, so there's genuine variety in the inventory, but the velocity at the median is what matters here. Buyers looking at Hillsdale are facing multiple offer situations on well-priced properties and will need to move decisively. The condo and townhouse tier is sparse with only three sales over the period, making it difficult to read as a meaningful market segment. If you're shopping in Hillsdale, you're really evaluating single-family homes, and you should expect competition from other buyers and sellers who know their pricing power.
